fact: October 10 is exactly 283 days into the year, which means there are only 82 days left until New Year's Eve!
In the Northern Hemisphere, squirrels are frantically burying nuts right now - they can bury up to 10,000 acorns in a single autumn!
The sun rises almost exactly due east and sets due west around this time of year, making it super easy to find directions without a compass.
Maple trees in cold regions are showing off their brightest red and orange colors right now because they're basically bleeding sugar!
October 10 falls smack in the middle of Libra season, which means the zodiac scales are perfectly balanced - just like autumn itself.
Monarch butterflies are flying over 2,000 miles south right now, and some of them are passing overhead today on their epic journey to Mexico.
The air pressure changes around this date make spider webs extra visible in the morning dew - it's like nature's own glitter bomb!
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere places, October 10 is when baby animals are being born everywhere because it's prime springtime.
Daylight is shrinking by about 3 minutes every single day in northern places, which means tomorrow will be noticeably darker than today.
Bears in cold climates are eating up to 20,000 calories per day right now - that's like eating 40 hamburgers every single day!
The moon looks extra huge and orange when it rises around this time of year because of all the harvest dust and particles floating in the air.
Geese flying south create those perfect V-shapes in the sky right around October 10, and the bird at the front has to work twice as hard as everyone else.
